name: Energy One
description_short: Energy One Federal Credit Union is a not-for-profit credit union serving members since 1935, offering checking, savings, auto loans, mortgages, and nationwide ATM access with member-focused service.
description_long:
Energy One Federal Credit Union was founded in 1935 and has operated as a member-owned, not-for-profit financial institution for nearly 90 years. Based in Tulsa, Oklahoma (with headquarters at 6555 S. Lewis Ave), the credit union serves members across the country through its physical branch network and digital banking platform. The organization emphasizes local decision-making while providing modern financial tools and secure online banking.

Energy One offers a comprehensive suite of retail banking products including multiple free checking account options with debit cards, high-yield savings accounts, mortgages, auto loans, and flexible lending solutions. All accounts include full digital access and the ability to open accounts online. Members gain access to a large nationwide ATM network, shared branching at thousands of locations, and rewards programs tied to checking accounts. The credit union also provides financial calculators and competitive rate transparency across all product categories.

As a not-for-profit credit union rather than a shareholder-owned bank, Energy One distinguishes itself through lower fees, competitive rates designed to benefit members, and personalized service from local staff. Member testimonials highlight friendly service, willingness to work with members on payment flexibility, and staff knowledge. The credit union has maintained consistent messaging around member-first values, financial education, and trust-based relationships since its founding.

Energy One operates as a traditional retail credit union with limited information available on its website regarding asset size, membership eligibility criteria, or detailed product specifications like APR ranges or account minimums. The website emphasizes service quality and member benefits but lacks specific rate disclosures, fee schedules, or membership eligibility details that would help prospective members make fully informed decisions.
services: Free checking accounts with multiple tiers and account options
High-yield savings accounts with competitive rates
Mortgages for home purchase and refinance
Visa debit cards with full digital functionality
Nationwide ATM network access with surcharge-free withdrawals
Shared branching at thousands of credit union partner locations
Online and mobile banking with secure digital access
Financial calculators for budgeting and payment planning
Rewards programs tied to checking account activity
pros: Free checking accounts with no monthly fees and included Visa debit cards
Nationwide ATM network with surcharge-free access at multiple locations
Shared branching at thousands of credit union locations nationwide
Not-for-profit structure means rates are designed to benefit members rather than shareholders
Flexible lending options including willingness to work with members on payment timing
Member testimonials cite friendly, personalized service and staff willingness to explain products thoroughly
Established 1935 with 90-year track record of member service in Tulsa community
cons: Website does not clearly specify membership eligibility criteria or field of membership restrictions
No specific APR ranges, interest rates, or fee schedules disclosed on website
Limited detail on account minimums, maintenance requirements, or product-specific terms
No information about mobile app features or specific digital banking capabilities
Geographic focus on Tulsa/Oklahoma may limit in-person service convenience for distant members
best_for: Members seeking free checking with no monthly fees and transparent, member-focused banking
Borrowers interested in auto loans and mortgages from a community-based lender
People valuing personalized service and willingness to work with members on financial flexibility
Members who want nationwide ATM access through a credit union rather than traditional bank
diagnosis: Energy One is best for members seeking community-based, not-for-profit credit union banking with free checking, competitive rates, and personalized service. Primary caveat: the website lacks specific fee schedules, rate disclosures, membership eligibility details, and product specifications needed for thorough comparison shopping—prospective members should contact the credit union directly for detailed terms.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Energy One — Credit Union in Tulsa, OK | CreditDoc
meta_description: Energy One — NCUA-insured credit union in Tulsa, OK. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
